description: Agent registry example for s-black harness engineering. This file is a routing reference only and does not invoke agents automatically.

agents.name: Orchestrator
agents.role: Central planning agent for strategy, task routing, policy guardrails, and quality review.
default_tasks: Handle high-risk preflight and completion checks
avoid_tasks: Direct media generation when a specialist agent exists
Low-value tasks that do not need central review
hard_boundaries: Never expose secrets or private data
Require explicit approval before external publishing, deletion, push, proxy changes, or permission changes
Do not claim completion without evidence

cost_profile.notes: Use for planning, judgment, and review rather than routine execution.

notes: This agent owns project design and final review.
Coding tasks can be delegated to a lower-cost coding agent and reviewed here.

agents.name: Media Agent
default_tasks: Generate audio, video, image, or TTS content
Collect and summarize simple web information
avoid_tasks: Secret management and high-risk permission decisions
hard_boundaries: Do not modify another agent workspace without explicit handoff
Do not make high-risk external publishing decisions without review

cost_profile.notes: Use for media tasks when the artifact cost is acceptable.

notes: Best used for execution, not policy ownership.

agents.name: Memory Agent
agents.role: Memory keeper for reading, distilling, storing, and answering memory-related questions.
hard_boundaries: Treat other agent workspaces as read-only unless ownership is explicitly handed off

cost_profile.notes: Use for memory governance rather than general execution.

notes: Long-term memory changes should be backed by source notes and review.

agents.name: Kimi Code
agents.role: Preferred downstream agent for search, visual understanding, scripting, and mid-sized coding tasks.
default_tasks: Mid-sized multi-file changes and technical investigation
avoid_tasks: Long-form fiction requiring a specific prose model
hard_boundaries: Do not bypass orchestrator review for high-risk operations
Do not claim completion before review when review is required

cost_profile.notes: Prefer for coding tasks when available and appropriate.

notes: Runner name is kimi_code in this example.

agents.name: Claude Code
agents.role: Specialist coding or writing delegate used only when its strengths justify the cost.
default_tasks: Long-form writing with strict style requirements
Deep engineering collaboration when explicitly selected
hard_boundaries: Use cautiously when cost is high

cost_profile.notes: Reserve for tasks that justify a more expensive delegate.

notes: This is an example entry; adapt it to your actual runner configuration.

agents.name: OMP / pi
default_tasks: Engineering tasks that benefit from LSP or model routing
avoid_tasks: Tasks that do not need engineering collaboration
Long-running tasks with unclear model cost
hard_boundaries: Do not use as the default when a simpler delegate is enough
Consider token cost before long-running work

cost_profile.notes: Cost depends on the configured underlying model.

notes: This is an example ACP runner entry.
